description: Schema: add `contracts.owner_client_id` for non-template client ownership
Types/API: add `owner_client_id` to contract interfaces and normal app create/update payloads
Migration: detect non-template contracts linked to more than one distinct client
Migration: choose the preserved assignment by invoice-history rule, otherwise earliest `start_date`
Migration: backfill `owner_client_id` onto preserved non-template contracts
Migration: clone contract header rows for non-preserved shared assignments with fresh `contract_id` values
Migration: clone `contract_lines` for cloned contracts with fresh `contract_line_id` values
Migration: clone contract-line child/configuration records needed for cloned contracts to remain self-contained
Migration: repoint non-preserved `client_contracts.contract_id` rows to their cloned contracts
Migration: preserve original `contract_id` for the single invoiced assignment when invoice history exists
Migration safety: abort shared-contract groups that require historical reference retargeting outside this pass
Guardrail: non-template contract creation requires an owning client in supported app flows
Guardrail: assignment create/update rejects attaching a non-template contract to a different client than `owner_client_id`
Guardrail: renewal-draft creation carries owner client to the new draft contract
Guardrail: legacy standalone contract create paths are aligned so they cannot recreate cross-client shared contracts
Contract model/actions: stop deriving live contract ownership semantics from shared contract assumptions
Contract model/actions: stop using aggregate multi-assignment logic to derive live `contracts.status` for client-owned contracts
Billing: keep `client_contracts -> contracts -> contract_lines` resolution but rely on owner-client invariant for unambiguous reads
Live contract status: standardize contract-facing status logic around assignment-derived lifecycle
Billing dashboard: reframe contracts hub so templates are reusable and non-template contracts are client-owned instances
ClientContractsTab: load rows from assignment-first data instead of contract-first list semantics
ClientContractsTab: render live status from `client_contracts` lifecycle, not `contracts.status`
Client contract list/actions: mutate assignment lifecycle for live contracts instead of contract-header status
Contract detail routing: use `clientContractId` as the primary live-contract context
Contract detail: show owner/assignment-derived live semantics instead of implying cross-client shared ownership
Templates UI: preserve template workflows as the only reusable contract-definition path
Contract report actions: pivot live contract fact sourcing from `contracts` to `client_contracts`
Revenue/expiration report definitions: stop counting active/live client contracts from `contracts.is_active`
API semantics: document and return `/contracts` as client-owned instantiated headers rather than reusable shared contracts
Docs: update contract-domain documentation to reflect templates-only reuse and client-owned non-template contracts
Client portal/document visibility: keep contract-linked document lookups correct for client-owned contracts after migration
Client contract line helpers: keep contract-backed overlap/listing queries correct under the owner-client invariant so contract-derived lines cannot bleed across clients
